q: In 1864, two Norwegian chemists proposed the ___ as a general description of the equilibrium condition.
The law of mass action is a ___ law for predicting the amounts of reactants and products.
According to the law of mass action, for a reversible reaction in a closed system, the ___ between the concentration of reactants and products is constant.
The rate of the forward reaction is directly ___ to the concentration of the reactants raised to a power equal to their coefficients.
The ratio $k_{f} / k_{r}$ is called the ___ constant.
The equilibrium constant $K_{eq}$ is a characteristic ___ value of the ratio of product concentrations to reactant concentrations.
The value of $K_{eq}$ is constant only at a specified ___.
The units of equilibrium constant depend on the form of the ___.
Pure solids or liquids are ___ from the equilibrium expression because their concentrations remain constant throughout the reaction.
If the $K_{eq}$ value is large, the equilibrium lies to the ___ and there are more products than reactants.
If the $K_{eq}$ value is small, the equilibrium lies to the ___ and there are more reactants than products.
If the $K_{eq}$ value is equal to ___, both reactants and products are equally favoured at equilibrium.
If molar concentrations are used, the equilibrium constant can be expressed as ___.
For equilibrium that involves gases, ___ can be used instead of concentration.
The equilibrium constant ___ can be given as the ratio of the partial pressures of products and reactants.
Because ___ is a pure liquid, its concentration is constant at a given temperature.
The equilibrium for the ___ of iodine crystals is an example of the solid-vapour heterogeneous equilibrium state.
The numerical value of the equilibrium constant is determined by measuring the ___ of each substance involved in a chemical reaction at equilibrium.
Equilibrium constant is a ratio that expresses the ___ between the amounts of products and reactants present at equilibrium.
When running on a treadmill, the person is in constant motion or ___ equilibrium.
